summary: James Mitchell convened a competitive strategy call with Rachel Torres, Tom Bradley, Jordan Whitfield, and Kevin O'Brien to address SentinelShield's aggressive positioning against Aegis in three recent late-stage deals. Tom Bradley explained the root cause and remediation of the March platform outage, confirming that redundant processing nodes and a circuit breaker pattern have been in production since March 28th. Jordan Whitfield highlighted Comply v2, launched April 7th, as a strong differentiator with native cross-module compliance reporting for SOC 2, PCI DSS, HIPAA, and ISO 27001. The team aligned on next steps for the at-risk Hartfield Manufacturing deal and identified materials needed to support the competitive response.
actionItems: Tom Bradley: Create a high-level technical architecture overview of the March incident remediation to share with prospects by end of next week
Jordan Whitfield: Complete updated competitive battlecard including CyberNova comparison, targeting end of next week
Rachel Torres: Schedule a technical call with Hartfield Manufacturing's infrastructure team for next week focused on Comply v2 and SOC 2 reporting
Kevin O'Brien: Attend Hartfield Manufacturing technical call and support the Comply v2 demo angle around their active SOC 2 audit

keyMoments.text: James reveals SentinelShield is actively using the March outage as a talking point in late-stage competitive deals, including with Hartfield Manufacturing
keyMoments.speaker: James Mitchell

keyMoments.text: Tom Bradley details the March outage root cause and confirms architectural remediation with redundant nodes and circuit breaker pattern is live in production
keyMoments.speaker: Tom Bradley

keyMoments.text: Jordan Whitfield positions Comply v2 as a native differentiator vs. bolt-on compliance in SentinelShield and VaultEdge, highlighting four-click report generation
keyMoments.speaker: Jordan Whitfield

keyMoments.text: Kevin O'Brien identifies Hartfield's active SOC 2 audit as an immediate pain point that on-demand SOC 2 reporting in Comply v2 can directly address
keyMoments.speaker: Kevin O'Brien
